item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Right before the audio logs there’s a leftover scp-xxxx

item.title: Site Criticism Team - Review
item.content:encoded:
Review requested via PM. So, let's get on with it.

+[Critique] Click to Access
-[Click to Hide Text]

There's already a few things that I don't care for at first glance. I don't think you need the strikethrough for the change of lead, nor do I think you need to bold or underline anything for emphasis. It isn't really downvote worthy, but it's something I tell people to avoid because of how unnecessary it is. Trust that your readers will pick up on those nuances.

For interview logs and the like, if there is something that isn't dialogue or a subheader, I recommend using italics, just so things don't get blurred together.

So this first log is pretty intense. Some of the dialogue is a bit hammy, like DarkStuff has mentioned, most of the liens being said by Sobakin. You could have left it at the first time that he shouts, say everything was too loud to hear properly, and told the rest of the log through describing the action. There's also a few points were you get into more descriptive, subjective terms like

[22:29]: Dr. Koshka manages to turn the camera up towards her face. Her eyes are wide with fear.

The device also captures Dr. Koshka's breathing, close to the mic, growing heavier, faster.

The female fighters tear into each other with ferocity

Save for that last one, which may be fine and I'm being overly picky, this isn't something you'd see in a clinical document, as they describe too heavily and too prose-like. If you removed the bolded parts, I think it would still get the point across as they rely on the context of the situation.

These newspaper excerpts and accompanying audio logs I think is where things start to dive down. You show a lot of your hand here, giving out a lot of information straight to the reader, even bringing in the tie to ancient ritual bluntly. It's stuff that you could have hinted at or even left out completely. We know what's happening now, so there isn't much more left to solve. And yet there is still one more log.

Overall, I'm not sure how to feel about this. I like the concept, no doubt about it. The way it's executed, though, has me swinging side to side.

The story you are trying to tell here, I think, is good. I like some of these beats that focus more on Koshka and her interactions with the Mothers and her relationship with Sobakin. It provides a lot of spotlight on the depth of the character Koshka. However, the news excerpts and some of the dialogue choices kind of irk me. I could have probably made some of the conclusions entirely from the interrogation log rather than the news excerpts, since it retreads some of that information.

As such, this is a downvote. A lot of the tension was lost in the middle, and it struggles to come back just in time to make the conclusion satisfying to me.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to RockTeethMothEyes or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: Re: Boop
item.content:encoded:
Hey DarkStuff,

Thanks for your feedback! Have reworked some of the easier fixes. Will take another look at the tone of those logs. Thanks again!

item.title: Boop
item.content:encoded:
I posted a critique in the forums just a little too late, it seems… sorry. But, what I said there echoes what I want to say here, so I'll link to it:

Here it is!

+1 still, but there are some things I would fix, tone wise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi there,

Thanks for your comments!

I think the problem was I wasn't going for comedy with that line. Haha. If you saw the piece as comedic then I apologise for the error. I can definitely look at the other places in the piece where the signalling for this might be off.

The line itself was said by Yuri because his mind is being taken over by a blood god. It's actually less of a D&D reference and more of a Warhammer one.

Thank you for voting though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey there,

Thanks to MacWarren and Amelia for your comments.

To me, it's from the perspective of a researcher who's trying her best to remain professional, but still coping with the murder of her husband. The line may not be clinical, but I felt it was justified in that context.

I'm sorry if it sounds cheesy/corny though. I can definitely try reworking the phrasing of some of the dialogue.

Thanks for voting!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Echoing MacWarren. The ending phrase is too cheesy and corny in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This would've been fine but that last sentence ruins the whole thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1.

I really liked this in a lot of places, but I really didn't like it in the places where it mattered. Primarily the dialogue. It's very hammy in a lot of areas and it takes away too much of the tension and intrigue for the sake of comedy or a "cool villain reveal" moment.

For example, I really enjoyed the first addendum until the, "BLOOD FOR THE BLOOD GOD!!" line. While I get it's for comedy, it killed (hah, get it?) the mood of the addendum and made Yuri seem less like a man attracted to senseless violence because of some primal human urge, and more like a D&D player who got way too invested into his Barbarian and is making the game uncomfortable for everyone else involved.

item.title: Thank you!
item.content:encoded:
there are vines that creep up through the gaps in my ribs, soft limbs of green to curl a cage

every sting, every snarl, every bite until the line between humanity and bloodlust is blurred with the plague painted in the air.

your words hurt the thread and needle butterfly, beating its wings faintly against the thorns cracking my bones into splinters.


+ poem credits:
- poem credits:

rice paper butterfly, jee
Used with permission, from:
https://hellopoetry.com/jee/

My third SCP article about MMA, decapitation and women warriors. It's not as bloody as you think. Well, maybe. [Warning: Some bloody images within].

Special thanks to the following people for trading honest and valuable critique along the way:
Dr_Aloe, AKFrost, CocaColalescence, Shadowknight_234, hi im stel, beegyoshi, kamelen800 and DrChandra.

+ Image Sources:
- Image Sources:
  • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:PLMMA_Cage-Octagon_MMA.JPG (CC BY-SA 4.0)
  • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Michele_Gutierrez_at_XFO_39_(2).jpg (CC BY-SA 2.0)
  • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Octagonal_Bath,_Hampi.jpg (CC BY-SA 3.0)
  • All news article images generated by me
